category_title: Preschoolers at risk for developmental delays with mild language disorders
scenario_title: Unstructured free play with minimal adult prompting
minimal_context: The student and a peer are playing with dramatic play toys and cars on a carpeted floor during a free play session.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: It is time for free play. You and your partner can play with the dramatic play toys and cars on the carpet.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Sits on the carpet, appearing lonely and making no comments to the peer.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What are you going to play?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looks at the peer) Can I have the car?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Does not engage in play with the peer) Can I have the car? Can I have the car? Can I have the car?

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: It is time for free play. You and your partner can play with the dramatic play toys and cars on the carpet.

STUDENT: (Sits on the carpet, appearing lonely and making no comments to the peer.)

TUTOR: What are you going to play?

STUDENT: (Looks at the peer) Can I have the car?

TUTOR: Keep playing together.

STUDENT: (Does not engage in play with the peer) Can I have the car? Can I have the car? Can I have the car?

task.content: Playing with toys (dramatic play, careers, or manipulative toys) during 10-minute baseline sessions
task.learning_goal: Producing peer-directed comments and verbal inquiries (requests)

context.environment: Space designated for project use on a carpeted floor
context.materials: Dramatic play toys (e.g., kitchen, grocery store, hospital), materials associated with careers (e.g., school bus drive, teacher, gardener, doctor), and manipulative toys (e.g., cars, blocks, trains)
context.social_setting: Dyad (two children) playing together with an interventionist present

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When the interventionist invited the children to play with the toys arranged on the carpeted floor and engaged in minimal conversation with the children while they played, and did not prompt language
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Decreased the frequency of comments to fewer than five comments in a session or no comments

behavior_patterns.condition: When attempting to make verbal inquiries or requests during the play session
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Having a high rate of requests for one session, however the reason for this high rate was due to asking the same question repeatedly

behavior_patterns.condition: When expected to interact with peers without structured support
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Demonstrated fewer social skills (e.g., making friends, following directions, initiating conversations with peers) and/or more problem behaviors (e.g. have temper tantrums)
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Appears lonely, shows anxiety, is sad or depressed
